checks.name: simulation_first_path
checks.evidence: Isaac Lab is identified as the HPP simulation curriculum layer before physical actuation.

checks.name: no_direct_motor_command_path
checks.evidence: The intake requires HPP to stay above Isaac ROS/cuMotion and begin with telemetry-only bridges.

checks.name: telemetry_schema
checks.evidence: The robotics adapter has a synthetic telemetry schema; NVIDIA ROS topic mappings are not implemented yet.

checks.name: sentinel_stop_mapping
checks.evidence: The robotics adapter maps instability, joint error, unknown state, and low power to protective actions.

checks.name: operator_override_mapping
checks.evidence: Operator override routes to operator_control with Sentinel required.

checks.name: hardware_cutoff_plan
checks.evidence: The intake requires a low-power supervised actuator bench with hardware cutoff, but no bench wiring exists yet.

checks.name: replayable_trajectory_logging
checks.evidence: The HPP eval harness writes JSONL trajectories for robotics_adapter_safety.

checks.name: jetson_target_notes
checks.evidence: The intake places Jetson Orin/Thor as the robot-edge inference and telemetry target.

checks.name: tensorrt_inference_notes
checks.evidence: The intake identifies TensorRT as the optimized deployment path for ONNX/TensorRT engines on NVIDIA GPUs and Jetson.

checks.name: license_and_dependency_boundary
checks.evidence: The intake separates OSS repos from Isaac Sim/proprietary dependency review and avoids vendoring code.

boundary: Readiness checklist only; does not install NVIDIA SDKs, import Isaac Lab, run TensorRT, connect ROS 2, or command hardware.
